The customers around me faithfully adhere to the etiquette of city dwellers, showing no interest at all when I walk into the bar.
Even though it's a familiar inside the bar, I strangely feel uneasy. Has this place stopped being my own?
They are staring at me. Are they trying to show sympathy, or are they keeping an eye on me in case I do something?
Today's
is different from usual; they are not busy with their hands but are staring at me intently.
Are you okay? Please keep your spirits up.
Well, it sounds like you think Jennifer is the culprit.
I believe it's almost certainly correct.
Please don't lose sight of the harsh reality, even if it's difficult.
Have you already gone to see Jennifer?
No, not yet.
I think you should hurry. She might notice.
The way you're saying it makes it sound like she is definitely the culprit.
I think she is currently the closest to being the culprit. Doesn't that make sense if you think about it realistically?
I understand how you feel, but...
Were you able to meet Jennifer?
No, she has already checked out of the hotel.
I see.
If we don't know where she's headed, she might indeed be the culprit.
I don't want to admit it, but it's becoming natural to think of her as the culprit.
Unfortunately, yes.
What on earth is she doing this for?
If the culprit is Jennifer, then the Decoy House might be clean by now.
Why?
I don't know why, but I feel like she's completely aware of our information.
It might just be your imagination.
When I asked the detective, he said the Decoy House was completely empty.
I see.
That's disappointing, but in that case, it looks like Jennifer was indeed the culprit.
It could be.
I wish we knew where she was headed.
Well, with this, we can’t help but think she’s the culprit.
That’s right. There is a possibility.
We can't rule out the chance that something might happen to her. We need to find her location quickly.
I know.
I get that, but how are we supposed to investigate?
We need to find out where Jennifer is.
The police are considering Meryl separately from the serial murder case. In reality, it’s suspicious that she could have killed Guy and Kristy.
Huh?
I don’t think Meryl did anything.
Then why?
Why hasn’t she been arrested yet?
Who knows?
That's something only she herself would know.
Unfortunately, the one currently considered the culprit is...
I think Jennifer is the culprit. After seeing that scene, it's not surprising that it's her room.
I know it's pointless to think about, but hearing the truth from Jennifer's mouth is terrifying.
Right before the police raided the Decoy House, she vacated the hotel. It seems too coincidental.
Where could she have gone?
Is there anyone who knows where Jennifer went?
The culprit brought the victim into that room and dismembered the body while she was still alive. Didn't the neighbors notice that at all?
They're indifferent to everyone else, showing no curiosity even when something happens next door.
Is that how it is?
Even if that dreadful creation made from the victim's body is gone, it’s certain the Decoy House is the crime scene.
How did the culprit manage to get ahead of the police and move that coffin?
By fleeing the hotel, Jennifer's position has worsened. Even I can't help but doubt her.
In any case, I hope there won't be any more victims. I have a feeling.
Knowing we witnessed that in the Decoy House, she moved the coffin before the police arrived.
Could she really have done something like that?
If Jennifer is the culprit, I wonder why she had to do such a thing. I'll have to hear it from her.
